2606.14612.title: Moonlight in Latent Space: Chirality and Structural Correspondence Between Beethoven's Op. 27 No. 2 and Machine Learning Mechanisms
2606.14612.paper_summary: We show that the three movements of Beethoven's "Moonlight Sonata" (Op. 27 No. 2) instantiate three distinct machine learning architectures -- not by analogy, but by structural correspondence. Through computational analysis of the score (entropy, Jensen-Shannon divergence, dissonance, hand distributional overlap, self-similarity matrices, temporal memory decay, and contextual pitch embeddings), we establish four counterintuitive findings: (1) perceived musical "temperature" is governed by throughput, not distributional width; (2) the lightest movement carries the highest dissonance; (3) the movements implement streaming, recurrent, and periodic positional encoding memory architectures; and (4) the same pitch class acquires different contextual identities across movements, analogous to contextual vs.static embeddings in NLP -- and unsupervised clustering recovers the tonal structure without music-theoretic input. We construct a reverse sonification (decoding analytical features back into MIDI) and quantify the chirality of the encode-decode cycle: what distributions preserve and sequential ordering destroys. Prompted by a listener's observation that the decoded piece sounds like "mirror isomers that can't be superimposed," the chirality measurement reveals reconstruction loss increasing monotonically with n-gram order. Bootstrap baselines and subsample checks confirm all movements carry sequential information above noise, though raw values are confounded by sample size. Cross-domain comparison shows natural language has higher chirality than music, reflecting stronger sequential constraints.
2606.14612.paper_summary_zh: 我們展示貝多芬的《月光奏鳴曲》（作品27第2號）的三個樂章體現了三種不同的機器學習架構——不是通過類比，而是通過結構對應。通過對樂譜的計算分析（熵、詹森-香農散度、不和諧、手部分佈重疊、自相似矩陣、時間記憶衰減和上下文音高嵌入），我們確立了四個反直覺的發現：（1）感知的音樂“溫度”由通量決定，而不是分佈寬度；（2）最輕的樂章承載著最高的不和諧；（3）這些樂章實現了流式、遞歸和周期性位置編碼記憶架構；（4）相同的音高類別在不同樂章中獲得不同的上下文身份，類似於自然語言處理中的上下文與靜態嵌入——而無監督聚類在沒有音樂理論輸入的情況下恢復了調性結構。我們構建了一個反向聲音化（將分析特徵解碼回MIDI），並量化編碼-解碼循環的手性：什麼分佈被保留，什麼序列排序被摧毀。受到聽眾觀察到解碼的作品聽起來像“無法重疊的鏡像異構體”的啟發，手性測量顯示重建損失隨著n-gram順序單調增加。自助基準和子樣本檢查確認所有樂章攜帶的序列信息超過噪音，儘管原始值受樣本大小的影響。跨領域比較顯示，自然語言的手性高於音樂，反映出更強的序列約束。
2606.14612.author: Chen Ying Claude et.al.
